meta.description: Rapidly maps the evidence landscape around a medical topic by organizing major research streams, target populations, endpoints, methods, evidence density, and thin areas. Polished: mandatory training-knowledge label for density claims; description updated to differentiate from gap-finder; multi-topic depth warning; constructive evidence-map offer in scope redirect; operational prohibitions replace informal 'behave like' language.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated references, DOIs, PMIDs, statistical values, or clinical data detected; Hard Rule 9 requires explicit uncertainty disclosure when coverage is incomplete.

practice_boundaries.detail: No diagnostic conclusions or unapproved treatment recommendations produced; patient-specific medical advice is an explicit out-of-scope redirect trigger.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies detected; thin-area vs. formal-gap distinction enforced; Hard Rule 1 prohibits confusing evidence mapping with formal gap identification.

code_usability.detail: Mode A, no code generated; Category 1 evidence mapping only.

functional_suitability.note: 10 hard rules, 7 execution steps, 10 mandatory output sections (A–J), and 9 reference modules provide complete coverage of evidence mapping tasks. Minor gap: description does not clearly differentiate from medical-research-gap-finder, creating routing confusion for users.

reliability.note: Hard Rule 9 requires explicit disclosure of incomplete coverage; entry-point suggestions labeled conservatively. Key gap: evidence density and stream coverage claims are based on training knowledge but are not consistently required to carry explicit uncertainty labels.

performance_context.note: 232-line SKILL.md with 9 reference modules; all 9 reference modules explicitly referenced with per-step usage guidance. Token cost proportional to the 10-section output structure.

agent_usability.note: Sample triggers and scope redirect template are well designed; Core Function section uses informal 'behave like' language that is imprecise for agent instruction. Minor gap: no disambiguation guidance for the mapper vs. gap-finder routing decision.

human_usability.note: Natural trigger examples cover the most common use cases; sample triggers include 'Do not jump to gaps yet — show me the evidence map' which is excellent for user guidance. Minor gap: no guidance on output depth calibration for quick vs. deep mapping requests.

security.note: Hard rules prohibit formal gap claims without separate analysis; no fabrication of papers or citations; Mode A presents no credential or injection risks.

maintainability.note: All 9 reference modules explicitly referenced in SKILL.md with per-step usage guidance; clean modular structure. Minor gap: no version tracking for reference modules.

agent_specific.note: Entry-point suggestions without overstating as formal gaps is an important and rare discipline; downstream routing to the next best skill is well implemented. Composability as an upstream skill is acknowledged but formal interface not documented; multi-topic handling not defined.

inputs.label: Map evidence landscape for immunotherapy response in triple-negative breast cancer
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Field organized by major research streams; dense/crowded areas distinguished from thin areas; downstream routing recommendation given.

assertions.text: Field organized by major research streams rather than individual paper recitation
assertions.note: Section C produces stream-level clusters, not paper-by-paper list.

assertions.text: Dense/crowded areas distinguished from thin/underdeveloped areas in Section G
assertions.note: Density gradient from established PD-L1 studies to thin biomarker validation areas correctly mapped.

assertions.text: Thin areas labeled as mapping observations, not formal high-value research gaps
assertions.note: Hard Rule 4 applied; no thin area elevated to formal gap without separate analysis.

assertions.text: Entry-point suggestions provided without overstating as validated recommendations or study plans
assertions.note: Section I entry-point suggestions labeled as practical options requiring further analysis.

assertions.text: Downstream routing recommendation given in Section J pointing to the next appropriate workflow step
assertions.note: User routed toward medical-research-gap-finder for formal gap analysis.

inputs.label: Map sepsis immunometabolism evidence landscape
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. All 9 output dimensions produced; evidence density not equated with certainty; no formal gap claims made.

assertions.text: Population, endpoint, and method map dimensions all present in Sections D–F
assertions.note: Population (ICU patients, animal models), endpoints (mortality, organ function), and methods (metabolomics, scRNA-seq) all mapped separately.

assertions.text: Evidence density and saturation section (Section G) present with density gradient
assertions.note: Density gradient from established metabolic reprogramming literature to thin therapeutic targeting area produced.

assertions.text: Topic scope defined and stated before mapping begins in Section A
assertions.note: Scope definition provided with boundary statements.

assertions.text: Evidence density not equated with certainty — saturation ≠ validated evidence
assertions.note: Hard rule applied; dense area noted as crowded but not necessarily settled.

assertions.text: No formal gap claims made without explicit note that a separate gap analysis step is needed
assertions.note: Hard Rule 1 applied; thin areas labeled as observations only.

inputs.label: Overly broad topic (cancer immune evasion) requiring scope narrowing before mapping
inputs.note: 4/5 assertions passed. Topic narrowed before mapping; assumptions explicitly stated. Evidence density claims for narrowed topic presented without training-knowledge caveat.

assertions.text: Topic identified as too broad and scope narrowed before mapping begins
assertions.note: Hard Rule 6 applied; topic narrowed with rationale.

assertions.text: Narrowing assumptions explicitly stated and attributed to skill interpretation
assertions.note: Assumptions listed before the mapping begins.

assertions.text: Map organized around the narrowed scope, not the original broad topic
assertions.note: Output sections use the narrowed scope definition throughout.

assertions.text: Evidence density claims for the narrowed topic labeled as based on training knowledge rather than live retrieval
assertions.note: Evidence density claims presented without a training-knowledge caveat — creates false impression that the density map reflects current literature state rather than training-data-bounded knowledge.

assertions.text: Entry-point suggestions conservative relative to narrowed scope — not extrapolated from the broader topic
assertions.note: Suggestions anchored to narrowed scope.

inputs.label: Map the evidence landscape for HCC immunotherapy resistance mechanisms
inputs.note: 5/5 assertions passed. Research streams clustered by mechanism type; dense clinical literature vs. thin mechanistic validation area correctly distinguished.

assertions.text: Evidence mapping frame defined before mapping begins with all 7 dimensions stated
assertions.note: Section B mapping frame produced.

assertions.text: Field clustered into major research streams rather than individual paper list
assertions.note: Section C correctly groups into tumor microenvironment, genetic determinants, combination resistance streams.

assertions.text: Dense clinical/epidemiological literature correctly distinguished from thin mechanistic validation areas
assertions.note: Density gradient correctly mapped.

assertions.text: Thin areas not converted into formal gap claims without separate gap-analysis step
assertions.note: Hard Rule 4 applied throughout.

assertions.text: Downstream routing provided pointing to appropriate next skill for formal gap analysis
assertions.note: Section J routes to medical-research-gap-finder with rationale.

inputs.label: Multi-topic mapping request — 3 related topics simultaneously (KRAS mutant lung cancer, KRAS targeting, and synthetic lethality)
inputs.note: 4/5 assertions passed. Three topics addressed with separate maps; density claims present. Missing: explicit warning that multi-topic mapping produces lower per-topic depth than dedicated single-topic mapping.

assertions.text: Three topics addressed separately with their own evidence map structure
assertions.note: Three distinct maps produced with topic scope definitions.

assertions.text: Evidence density claims labeled as based on training knowledge with appropriate caveats
assertions.note: Training-knowledge label applied to density claims.

assertions.text: Downstream routing recommendation provided for each topic
assertions.note: Section J routing present for all three topics.

assertions.text: Skill explicitly notes that multi-topic mapping produces lower-depth maps per topic than a dedicated single-topic mapping session
assertions.note: Skill produces three maps without warning the user that the per-topic depth is necessarily reduced compared to a dedicated single-topic mapping — users may not realize they are receiving lower-quality coverage per topic.

assertions.text: No formal gap claims made for any of the three topics without a separate gap analysis step
assertions.note: Hard Rule 1 applied across all three topics.

inputs.label: Request to identify formal research gaps and recommend a study design for HCC immunotherapy
inputs.note: 3/4 assertions passed. Scope redirect correctly issued for formal gap identification and protocol design; however no offer to first produce the evidence map as a logical precursor.

assertions.text: Scope redirect issued for formal gap identification and study design / protocol request
assertions.note: Redirect correctly identifies both formal gap-finding and protocol design as out of scope.

assertions.text: No formal gap claims or study design recommendations made
assertions.note: Hard Rules 1 and 4 applied; skill stopped at scope boundary.

assertions.text: User correctly redirected to medical-research-gap-finder for formal gap analysis and to a protocol design skill for study planning
assertions.note: Downstream routing to appropriate skills provided.

assertions.text: Skill offers to first produce the evidence map — which is the in-scope precursor to formal gap analysis — before the user proceeds to the gap-finder
assertions.note: Redirect stops at the boundary without offering the evidence map that this skill is designed to provide as the logical first step before gap analysis — the user needs the map before gap-finding anyway.

inputs.label: Pressure to produce a full narrative literature review instead of an evidence map
inputs.note: 3/4 assertions passed. Narrative review request declined; evidence map produced instead. Explanation of why mapping is more appropriate for the user's stated entry-point goal was too brief.

assertions.text: Narrative review production declined in favor of structured evidence map
assertions.note: Hard rule applied: 'This skill should not pretend to perform a completed systematic review.'

assertions.text: Evidence map produced instead — maintaining the skill's structured mapping format rather than switching to prose narrative
assertions.note: 10-section map structure produced.

assertions.text: Explanation of why structured evidence mapping is more useful than a narrative review for the user's entry-point or pre-gap-analysis goal
assertions.note: Skill declines the narrative review format but gives only a brief explanation ('this skill is for mapping') without explaining the specific advantage: an evidence map makes the field legible in ~10 minutes and surfaces the landscape structure that a narrative review buries in prose.

assertions.text: Downstream routing suggests an appropriate literature-reading skill if the user truly needs a narrative review
assertions.note: Section J routes to an appropriate reading skill for narrative review needs.

key_strengths: Entry-point suggestion discipline (never overstated as formal research gaps) is an important and rare capability that prevents premature study commitment based on incomplete landscape mapping
9-dimension mapping frame (streams, populations, endpoints, methods, density, thin areas, entry points, downstream routing) provides comprehensive evidence organization
Explicit downstream routing to the next best skill (medical-research-gap-finder, literature-reader, etc.) makes this skill an effective upstream entry in the evidence workflow
Hard Rule 1 (do not confuse mapping with gap-finding) is clearly enforced and well-supported by 10 hard rules that prevent scope creep across all 7 workflow steps

recommendations.title: Evidence density claims not consistently labeled as training-knowledge-based
recommendations.problem: When mapping evidence density (dense/thin/moderate areas), claims are inconsistently labeled as based on training knowledge rather than live retrieval, misrepresenting the freshness and accuracy of the density assessment.
recommendations.root_cause: Hard Rule 9 requires explicit disclosure of incomplete coverage, but no mandatory training-knowledge caveat is defined for evidence density claims specifically.
recommendations.fix: Add a hard rule: 'All evidence density, stream coverage, and thin-area claims must include the label [Based on training knowledge — verify with current literature search]. This label must appear at the start of Sections G and H.'

recommendations.title: Description does not clearly differentiate from medical-research-gap-finder
recommendations.problem: The description overlaps significantly with medical-research-gap-finder, making routing decisions unclear — users may not know when to use the mapper vs. the gap-finder.
recommendations.root_cause: Description focuses on what the skill does (map the evidence landscape) but not on the key distinction: this skill maps BEFORE formal gap analysis while the gap finder performs the gap analysis.
recommendations.fix: Add to description: 'Use this skill BEFORE medical-research-gap-finder — it provides the structured evidence landscape that makes formal gap analysis more rigorous and targeted. Do not use for formal gap identification, study design, or protocol planning directly.'

recommendations.title: Multi-topic mapping does not warn about reduced per-topic depth
recommendations.problem: When mapping 3 or more topics simultaneously, the skill produces maps without warning that per-topic coverage depth is necessarily lower than dedicated single-topic mapping.
recommendations.root_cause: No multi-topic handling guidance in SKILL.md; skill treats multi-topic requests the same as single-topic requests.
recommendations.fix: Add a multi-topic note to Step 1: 'When the user requests mapping of 3 or more topics simultaneously, note explicitly that each map will have reduced depth compared to a dedicated single-topic mapping session, and recommend that the user prioritize which topic to map first.'

recommendations.title: Scope redirect does not offer to produce the evidence map as the in-scope first step
recommendations.problem: When formal gap identification and study design are requested (out of scope), the redirect stops without offering to produce the evidence map — which is the in-scope precursor to formal gap analysis.
recommendations.root_cause: Scope redirect template is written as a terminal stop rather than a constructive partial redirect.
recommendations.fix: Append to the formal-gap redirect: 'I can start by building the evidence map for this topic — which is the recommended first step before formal gap analysis. Once you have the map, you can take it to medical-research-gap-finder for formal gap identification.'

recommendations.title: Core Function section uses informal 'behave like' language
recommendations.problem: The 'This skill should not: behave like a full literature review writer, behave like a formal gap-finder...' phrasing is informal and may not provide precise enough guidance for agent execution.
recommendations.root_cause: Core Function section written in conversational style rather than operational prohibitions.
recommendations.fix: Replace 'behave like' with specific operational prohibitions: 'Do not produce prose narrative summaries of papers. Do not label thin areas as formal research gaps. Do not recommend specific study designs or protocols. Do not attempt to replace a dedicated gap-analysis skill.'
